item.title: Re: Who's a good boy?
item.content:encoded: I’m glad you liked it. Yeah I wouldn’t add pictures because of what you described as well as to preserve the narrative structure of the reveal.

item.title: Re: Why is this at -5?
item.content:encoded: I also think the initial heavy downvotes were due to errors I missed where the guy's son was a daughter in a few sentences for some reason. Otherwise, yeah, the style needs a tune-up. I'm not a horror writer, so I'm relying on critiques from better horror writers to help. I'm glad it was still enjoyable!

item.title: Why is this at -5?
item.content:encoded: It could use a style tune-up, but it's a great idea and storyline!

item.title: Re: Who's a good boy?
item.content:encoded:
This is great!
Please don't add pictures. That would undermine the "sincerity" of it, as seeing a picture is supposed to have an effect.

item.title: Staff Post - Deletion Vote
item.content:encoded:
Beginning deletion vote at -10.

Article has since moved to -9. Deletion timer removed. ~ Conwell

If you are not the author and you want to rewrite this article, you may reply to this post asking for the opportunity to do so. Please obtain permission from the author (or the Rewrite Team if this article is older than 6 months) and make sure you copy the page source to your sandbox. Please do not reply to this post for any other reason unless you are staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Considering the brutal honesty I’ve seen on other failed first attempts, I’m still floored at the positive reaction I’m getting. I’m really glad people feel I got close and hope to workshop this until it’s something special.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll second what Pepper said. Close, but not there yet

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would definitely appreciate any critiques. I already caught one accidental error and fixed it and there’s probably other stuff I was blind to. Plus I don’t usually write horror. This was a personal challenge and while I’m satisfied with how it turned out, I’m well aware that it’s probably amateurish. It’s better than I thought I would do but I definitely would appreciate help polishing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels like it's one or two rounds of editing away from being a really solid article, but it's not quite there yet. I'll go ahead and volunteer to provide critique if you decide to take another shot at this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's perfectly valid. I had it looked over a few times, but it never hurts to have it looked over even more and make further adjustments. If it's still up in a week's time, I'll probably give it another once over. Stepping away for a bit is usually a good way for me to spot places I can improve.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the tone and writing could use a little bit of a tune up, but that's to be expected of a first timer. I really like the idea, and think it's solid. Just maybe get an experienced author to give it a once over.

item.title: Who's a good boy?
item.content:encoded:
Hey everyone, I hope you enjoy my first SCP. This SCP was based on a short story I wrote several years ago which in turn was based on the SCP universe, so I've come full circle. Also, if my girlfriend is reading this: there's no need to read the article, and all the dogs are completely fine and get good homes.

Anyways, feel free to let me know how I could improve it and any general thoughts.

Sincerely,

Norm L. Manne
